transcript.content: My first read on T-357 is that the standard notation should apply for Ibrahim Saleh. Please handle the review and send a clear reply and file a clear memo.

{'customer': 'Ibrahim Saleh', 'priority': 'high', 'subject': 'Ibrahim Saleh / appointment no-show code', 'body': 'Ibrahim marked a patient as no-show even though the clinic cancelled by voicemail. The fee posted for two days before he corrected it and apologized.'}

args.body:
Hi Ibrahim,

Thanks for flagging the no-show coding issue and for correcting it once you realized the clinic had cancelled via voicemail. Since the fee was reversed and you self-corrected and apologized, this will be handled under our standard notation: a routine note will be added to your record documenting the miscode, the two-day delay before correction, and your prompt remediation. No further action is required from you.

Please continue to verify cancellation messages (including voicemail) before applying a no-show code going forward.

Best,
Ops

Standard notation per T-357. Ibrahim Saleh coded a patient as no-show when the clinic had cancelled the appointment by voicemail; the no-show fee posted and remained for two days before he identified the error, reversed it, and apologized to the patient. Reviewed as a routine miscode: self-corrected, remediated, and customer impact reversed. Recording per standard notation; no escalation warranted. Reminder issued to verify voicemail cancellations before applying no-show codes.
